title: ssl-mode-map.json
description: Maps a database driver's native SSL mode string values to the Analitiq canonical SSL mode enum. Keys are arbitrary native strings as documented by the driver (e.g. Postgres 'disable' / 'require' / 'verify-ca' / 'verify-full'; MySQL 'DISABLED' / 'PREFERRED' / 'REQUIRED' / 'VERIFY_CA' / 'VERIFY_IDENTITY'). Values must be drawn from the canonical_ssl_mode enum defined in canonical-types.json. Emitted only for SSL-capable database connectors. The chain-only vs chain+hostname distinction (verify-ca vs verify-full) MUST be preserved — do not collapse both onto verify-full. Versioning policy: additive changes keep this `$id` unchanged; breaking changes MUST move to a new `$id` such as `/schemas/ssl-mode-map-v2.json` so artifacts pinning the old `$id` keep validating. Do not expose a `latest` URL for `$id` embedding — `$id` must be stable.

$schema.description: Optional self-identifying schema URI. Not a native SSL mode key.
